summary_oneline: Add provider-agnostic prompt caching: extend PromptBuilder with cacheable segments, teach each AbstractClient subclass to translate them to native cache primitives, and ship an AGENT_CONTEXT.md loader with mtime invalidation for repo-level context injection.

localization.role: Composable system-prompt builder with CONFIGURE/REQUEST two-phase render — the natural home for cacheable-segment output and the `prompt_caching` flag.
Public API surface where any new PromptBuilder methods/presets must be re-exported.
Preset registry that may grow a `agent_with_context` preset if AGENT_CONTEXT_LAYER ships as built-in.
Base class with complete/ask/ask_stream; system_prompt is `Optional[str]` today. Receives a new optional cache-hint plumbing.
13+ call sites that build `payload['system'] = system_prompt`. Targets for cache_control block injection via a single helper.
OpenAI call sites; auto-caches on ≥1024-token prefixes. Optional `prompt_cache_key` for cache-routing improvements.
Primary Gemini call sites; requires explicit `CachedContent` resource creation before reuse — asymmetric with Anthropic.
Additional Gemini call sites used by analysis flows; cache hooks must cover them too.
Generation-side Gemini call sites; same translation surface.
OpenAI-compatible API; Groq does not document prompt caching as of this proposal — degrades to no-op.
xAI Grok via OpenAI-compatible shape; same no-op stance as Groq for v1.
Already accepts `prompt_builder` and legacy `system_prompt`. Receives a new `prompt_caching: bool = False` kwarg and propagates to client at call time.
Constructs PromptBuilder.agent() by default; benefits from the new flag with zero subclass changes.
Canonical consumer that motivates this work. Today on the legacy `system_prompt=` path; will opt in via `prompt_caching=True` and an AGENT_CONTEXT layer for the repo doc.
Home for a new `AGENT_CONTEXT_DIR = config.get('AGENT_CONTEXT_DIR', fallback=BASE_DIR.joinpath('agent_context'))` following the established pattern.
Reference pattern for the AGENT_CONTEXT.md mtime-based in-memory cache.
Reference pattern when/if the AGENT_CONTEXT loader is called from async context (the codebase explicitly forbids `functools.lru_cache` on async methods).

constraints.statement: PromptBuilder.build() returns a single string today; the rest of the codebase relies on that signature.
constraints.implication: Add a new method (e.g. `build_segments() -> List[CacheableSegment]`) instead of changing the existing return type. Default unchanged paths produce a single non-cacheable segment so legacy callers see no behavior change.

constraints.statement: AbstractClient.complete()/ask()/ask_stream() accept `system_prompt: Optional[str]` and the contract was just stabilized via the homologate-llm-clients-askstream initiative.
constraints.implication: Do NOT change the existing signature. Add a sibling optional parameter (e.g. `system_prompt_segments`) or accept `Union[str, List[CacheableSegment]]` so legacy callers keep passing strings. The cache plumbing must compose with the lifecycle events emitted by `_emit_before_call` / `_emit_after_call`.

constraints.statement: Anthropic's `messages.create` requires the system field to switch from a string to a list of content blocks to attach `cache_control`. Limit of 4 cache_control blocks per request.
constraints.implication: Per-call translation must aggregate cacheable segments into ≤4 blocks; the first cacheable block(s) get `cache_control={'type':'ephemeral'}`, the rest stay un-marked rather than spilling over the limit.

constraints.statement: Gemini's `CachedContent` is a separate API resource created BEFORE `generate_content` and has a minimum token threshold (≥4096 for most models, ≥32k for newer Flash variants).
constraints.implication: Translation must be conditional: estimate token count, skip the cache and log at debug level when the threshold is not met. The feature must be 'best-effort' per provider — never raise.

constraints.statement: GitHubReviewer's default model is Gemini-3-Flash, the provider with the highest cache-minimum threshold.
constraints.implication: The consumer that motivated this feature may not actually cache on its default model unless AGENT_CONTEXT.md (+ static system prompt) exceeds the threshold. Document this honestly; consider Anthropic/OpenAI for the canonical demo.

constraints.statement: navconfig + BASE_DIR + `config.get('X_DIR', fallback=BASE_DIR.joinpath('x'))` is the established convention for project paths.
constraints.implication: Declare `AGENT_CONTEXT_DIR` (or chosen name) in `parrot/conf.py` and use `fallback=` (NOT `default=`) per the navconfig Kardex convention recorded in memory.

If the AGENT_CONTEXT loader is ever called from async code, use the module-level sync `@functools.lru_cache` on the inner read function and wrap with `asyncio.to_thread(...)`, or use the async-safe LRU pattern.

constraints.statement: The lifecycle events system (FEAT-176) is now integrated into AbstractClient with privacy-safe `_system_prompt_hash`.
constraints.implication: Emit new `PromptCacheAppliedEvent` / `PromptCacheSkippedEvent` (reason: 'below_threshold' | 'provider_unsupported' | 'feature_off') inside the same emit-before/after lifecycle so subscribers can attribute cache cost/benefit per call.

constraints.statement: Zero prior prompt-caching code exists in `parrot/` (green field) — no migration constraints.
constraints.implication: The proposal can design the cleanest API surface. The flip side: there is no prior art in-repo to course-correct against; the spec must be precise.

new.item: CacheableSegment dataclass — `(text: str, cacheable: bool, ttl_hint: Optional[Literal['short','long']] = None)` representing a chunk of the system prompt with a cache-eligibility flag.
PromptBuilder.build_segments(context) → List[CacheableSegment] that derives cache boundaries from layer.phase (RenderPhase.CONFIGURE = cacheable, REQUEST = not).
PromptLayer.cacheable: bool attribute (default True for CONFIGURE-phase, False for REQUEST-phase) — overridable per layer for fine control.
`prompt_caching: bool` flag on PromptBuilder + on AbstractBot.__init__ (default False to keep existing behavior identical).
PromptCachingMixin (or abstract method) on AbstractClient: `_translate_cache_hints(payload, segments) -> payload` that each subclass overrides for its native primitive.
Anthropic translator: aggregates cacheable segments into ≤4 system blocks with `cache_control={'type': 'ephemeral'}`; preserves the single-string payload contract when feature is off.
OpenAI translator: emits a deterministic `prompt_cache_key` derived from a SHA-256 of the cacheable segments (mirrors the pattern of `_system_prompt_hash`); leaves the messages list shape unchanged.
Gemini translator: estimates token count, calls `client.caches.create(...)` when ≥ provider threshold, passes `cached_content=<name>` to generate_content; skips with debug log when below threshold.
AGENT_CONTEXT_DIR constant in parrot/conf.py following the BASE_DIR `fallback=` pattern.
AgentContextLoader: sync file read with mtime-based @lru_cache invalidation (one cache entry per path). Loads `AGENT_CONTEXT.md` from `AGENT_CONTEXT_DIR` (or per-agent override).
AGENT_CONTEXT_LAYER: a CONFIGURE-phase, cacheable PromptLayer that lazily reads the doc on configure(). Available as opt-in, not in `default` preset.
Lifecycle events: `PromptCacheAppliedEvent(provider, blocks, est_tokens)` and `PromptCacheSkippedEvent(provider, reason)` emitted by AbstractClient before the SDK call.
new.location_hint: packages/ai-parrot/src/parrot/telemetry or wherever existing events live

new.item: Tests: per-provider unit tests assert (a) feature-off path produces identical payload to today, (b) feature-on path produces the correct native primitive, (c) below-threshold case for Gemini emits a debug log + no crash.

modified.change: Add `prompt_caching: bool` constructor arg, `build_segments()` method, and a `is_caching_enabled` property; existing `build()` unchanged.
Add `cacheable: bool` attribute to PromptLayer; default derives from phase.
Accept `prompt_caching: bool = False` kwarg; forward to builder; thread segments through to client via `ask()/ask_stream()`.
Add optional `system_prompt_segments` parameter (or accept Union[str, List[CacheableSegment]]) on complete/ask/ask_stream; default behavior unchanged for str inputs.
Wrap the 13+ `payload['system'] = system_prompt` sites behind a `_apply_system_prompt(payload, system_prompt, segments)` helper that conditionally produces the cache_control list form.
Compute and pass `prompt_cache_key` when segments are provided; no shape change to messages list.
Add a Gemini CachedContent helper invoked from generate_content paths; conditional on segment-token estimate ≥ provider threshold.
Route generate_content call sites through the same helper.
Route generate_content call sites through the same helper.
Opt in: `prompt_caching=True` + add AGENT_CONTEXT_LAYER to the per-repo subclass. Document the Gemini-threshold caveat in the class docstring.

untouched_non_goals: Loaders, vectorstores, integrations (Telegram/Slack/MS Teams/MCP) — out of scope.
Client-side caching of response bodies (only provider-side cache hints in v1).
Multi-tenant per-user cache-key segmentation in v1 (single global key is fine).
Adding cache support to providers that do not document it (Groq, Grok, HuggingFace, NVIDIA, LocalLLM, vLLM, OpenRouter) — these degrade to no-op.
Migrating GithubReviewer off the legacy `system_prompt=` path to a full PromptBuilder; the AGENT_CONTEXT layer is added on top of the existing approach.
Caching tool definitions (Anthropic supports cache_control on tools but v1 caches only the system block).
patterns_to_follow: PromptBuilder two-phase render (CONFIGURE/REQUEST) maps naturally to cacheable/non-cacheable boundaries — F001.
AbstractClient `_system_prompt_hash` privacy pattern for emitting events without leaking content — F002.
navconfig + BASE_DIR + `fallback=` for the AGENT_CONTEXT_DIR — F007.
kb/local.py mtime-based file invalidation for AgentContextLoader — F008.
registry/routing/cache.py async-safe LRU when the loader is called from async — F008.
EventEmitterMixin + lifecycle events (FEAT-176) for cache telemetry — F009.
integration_risks: Anthropic's 4-block cache_control limit: if AGENT_CONTEXT + system_prompt + tool defs each get marked, v1 may overshoot. Mitigation: v1 caches at most 1-2 blocks (AGENT_CONTEXT + identity-section).

Gemini's high cache threshold (4096/32k) means GithubReviewer (the consumer) may not actually cache on its default model. Mitigation: document honestly; emit PromptCacheSkippedEvent so cost/benefit is observable.
Recent client homologation just stabilized — adding parameter to ask_stream must not regress the AIMessage final-yield contract (F009).

PromptBuilder is used by Voice/Agent/RAG presets and downstream agents (jira_specialist, database/agent.py, data.py); the new `build_segments()` must coexist with `build()` to avoid touching ~10 callers.

confidence_map.statement: A fully-featured PromptBuilder exists at parrot/bots/prompts/builder.py with two-phase CONFIGURE/REQUEST rendering.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of the file; class definition and presets verified.

confidence_map.statement: Anthropic's payload assigns `payload['system'] = system_prompt` as a plain string at 13+ call sites in claude.py.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct grep yielded explicit line numbers; payload assembly verified for the primary call site (188-235).

confidence_map.statement: OpenAI/Groq/Grok use chat.completions.create / responses.create with the system prompt inside the messages list.
confidence_map.reasoning: Grep returned all the call sites; payload shape is OpenAI-standard.

confidence_map.statement: Gemini uses self.client.aio.models.generate_content without any caching today.
confidence_map.reasoning: Grep across google/*.py covers all call sites; second grep for cached_content/CachedContent returned nothing.

confidence_map.statement: AbstractBot already accepts both `system_prompt` and `prompt_builder` kwargs; the builder takes precedence when set.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of __init__ signature and body (lines 247-309).

confidence_map.statement: GitHubReviewer uses the legacy `system_prompt=` path with a static _SYSTEM_PROMPT and does not load any repo-level context document.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of github_reviewer.py:383-387 (kwargs.setdefault) and 941-958 (per-review question assembly).

confidence_map.statement: GitHubReviewer's default model is Gemini-3-Flash-Preview, the provider with the highest cache-minimum threshold.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of `model = GoogleModel.GEMINI_3_FLASH_PREVIEW` at line 269.

confidence_map.statement: No prior prompt-caching code exists in packages/ai-parrot/src/parrot/.
confidence_map.reasoning: Repo-wide grep for the canonical caching identifiers returned zero matches.

confidence_map.statement: No AGENT_CONTEXT.md-like loader exists in the codebase today.
confidence_map.reasoning: Grep for AGENT_CONTEXT/context_file/context_path returned no relevant loader.

confidence_map.statement: navconfig + BASE_DIR + `config.get(..., fallback=BASE_DIR.joinpath(...))` is the established convention for project directories.
confidence_map.reasoning: parrot/conf.py contains 8+ such declarations following this exact pattern.

confidence_map.statement: Mtime-based file invalidation is a precedent in the codebase (stores/kb/local.py).
`functools.lru_cache` silently misbehaves on async methods; the project has a custom async-safe LRU.
confidence_map.reasoning: Module docstring of registry/routing/cache.py states this explicitly.

confidence_map.statement: The AbstractClient API surface has just been homologated and is currently in its cleanest state in months.
confidence_map.reasoning: Recent commits show 8+ tasks completing the ask_stream homologation; 'stable' is inferred from the absence of in-flight refactors in the last 60 days, not from explicit announcement.

confidence_map.statement: Lifecycle events from FEAT-176 are the right substrate for cache-applied / cache-skipped telemetry.
confidence_map.reasoning: EventEmitterMixin is integrated into AbstractClient; emitting two new event types should compose, but we have not read the EventBus to confirm subscription semantics for new event classes.

confidence_map.statement: Anthropic enables prompt caching via system: [{type:'text', text:'...', cache_control:{type:'ephemeral'}}] with a limit of 4 cache_control blocks per request.
confidence_map.reasoning: Well-documented externally by Anthropic; no in-repo evidence (zero prior caching code per F006). User input may refine the exact constants.

confidence_map.statement: Gemini's CachedContent has a minimum token threshold of 4096 (or higher for some Flash variants).
confidence_map.reasoning: Well-documented externally; no in-repo evidence. The exact threshold per model is documented by Google and may shift; v1 must be resilient to a configurable threshold.

unknowns.question: Where should AGENT_CONTEXT.md live by default — repo root (BASE_DIR/AGENT_CONTEXT.md), a dedicated dir (BASE_DIR/agent_context/AGENT_CONTEXT.md, configurable via AGENT_CONTEXT_DIR), or per-agent (agents/<agent_id>/AGENT_CONTEXT.md)?
plausible_answers: Repo root: simplest, one doc per deployment, matches the GitHubReviewer 'one doc per reviewed repo' mental model.
AGENT_CONTEXT_DIR with per-agent files: scales to multiple agents/repos in one deployment, matches the existing AGENTS_DIR convention.
Per-agent inside AGENTS_DIR/<agent_id>/: zero new top-level config, leverages an existing path.
unknowns.user_answer: AGENT_CONTEXT_DIR (per-agent files) — AGENT_CONTEXT_DIR = config.get('AGENT_CONTEXT_DIR', fallback=BASE_DIR.joinpath('agent_context')) with one file per agent (<dir>/<agent_id>.md).

unknowns.question: Should `prompt_caching` be a boolean flag or a richer enum like `'auto' | 'force' | 'off'`?
plausible_answers: Boolean True/False — simplest; provider degrades silently when unsupported or below threshold.
Enum 'auto'/'force'/'off' — 'force' raises if a provider can't satisfy, 'auto' degrades silently. More expressive but heavier API surface.
unknowns.user_answer: Boolean True/False — silent degradation via PromptCacheSkippedEvent; an enum can be added later if a 'force' semantic is needed.

unknowns.question: Should the AGENT_CONTEXT_LAYER ship as part of the `default` preset (everyone gets it for free, doc may not exist → empty layer) or only as an opt-in preset (`agent_with_context`)?
plausible_answers: Opt-in preset only — explicit; no surprise overhead for agents that don't want it.
Part of default — set-and-forget; layer renders empty when doc is missing.
Auto-include when prompt_caching=True — couples the layer to the cache flag, which has clean ergonomics for the GitHubReviewer case.
unknowns.user_answer: Auto-include when prompt_caching=True — single switch activates both cache plumbing and the AGENT_CONTEXT layer. If the per-agent file doesn't exist, the layer renders empty (with a one-time INFO log on configure()).

recommended_next_command.rationale: Localization is high-confidence (17 verified paths), the patterns to follow are explicit, and there is no architectural fork — only tactical decisions (default doc location, flag shape, preset policy) that can be locked in by the spec author. No prior art to migrate, but a clear seam in PromptBuilder and AbstractClient to extend.

meta.notes: Two medium-confidence claims (C16, C17) rest on external Anthropic/Google documentation rather than in-repo evidence — explicit by design since F006 confirmed no prior caching code. The spec phase should pin the exact constants per provider as named constants in a single module so they can be updated independently.
